verses.text: When the tabernacle was reared, the princes brought six wagons and twelve oxen. The gifts. Leaders give for the service.
When the tabernacle was reared, the princes brought six wagons and twelve oxen. The gifts. Leaders give for the service.
When the tabernacle was reared, the princes brought six wagons and twelve oxen. The gifts. Leaders give for the service.
God bids Moses receive them for the Levites' work. Received. The gifts are put to use.
God bids Moses receive them for the Levites' work. Received. The gifts are put to use.
God bids Moses receive them for the Levites' work. Received. The gifts are put to use.
The wagons go to Gershon and Merari; Kohath bears on the shoulder. Distributed. Each bears according to his charge.
The wagons go to Gershon and Merari; Kohath bears on the shoulder. Distributed. Each bears according to his charge.
The wagons go to Gershon and Merari; Kohath bears on the shoulder. Distributed. Each bears according to his charge.
At the dedication, the princes offer, each on his own day. Dedication. One leader honours God each day.
At the dedication, the princes offer, each on his own day. Dedication. One leader honours God each day.
Each prince brings the same offering — silver chargers, gold spoons, and beasts. Alike. No tribe outdoes another in honouring God.

The sum of all the princes' offerings is reckoned. The total. The whole nation's gift is recorded.

Moses hears God's voice from off the mercy seat. The voice. God speaks from between the cherubim.
